title: Is SEELE really off the table
display_title: Is SEELE really off the table
wikitext:
[]: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 04, 2013 10:05 pm The common consesus here seems to be that after Gendo took the old men's brains of their life support, he's to be seen as having tricked them out and taken their comfy supervillain chair, but I've always seen the context here very differently. Even before, Gendo & Fuyu were discussing how SEELE didn't need to discuss stuff with them/directly act anymore; They left behind everything already set into motion, rolling down the paths they want it on with every passing day. Everything was already set into place, and Fuyutsuki's final resumen makes it look like their faction is by far not out of worries: "It nearly went all as SEELE intended" - In fact, the old men pose most of their direct threat after their deaths, through the nifty programs they left behind in the Mk 9 and its attempt to take over the Wunder - Without it, WILLE might as well have been able to blow EVA 13 clean out of the sky. Seeing SEELE as finished seems to disregard a few fundamental truths of what SEELE is: First, they have always, always primarily acted through indirect, cold, unpersonal means, like mechanical or montrous tools, or faceless, ruthless goons. MP EVAS, JSSDF Soldiers, mib agents... so it's not very surprising that they've become brain jars, it goes so well with their despising of the everyday human sphere with eating and interacting and everything; Freed from those needs, they themselves have become mechanized, Keel's implants taken to their logical conclusion. They had the Mk Nine; They probably have other tools, they obviously have the nemesis series; Even with the men themselves dead, SEELE has the means to pose a tangible threat from beyond the grave; The good guys will have sufficient quantities of things to smash. Also, SEELE is and always have been essentially a suicide cult; Their big words are "death" and "purification"; They're a suicide cult, and they want the whole wordld to come with them. And we know that they had to change the original planned ending top something more... abstrtact after the whole Aum business, so what we got were the MP EVas stabbing themselves and other similar goodies.Bbut now, after years have passed and the situation has cooled, they can show SEELE doing an actual mass-suicide in all of it's creepy glory. But they're essentially... going on ahead to the promised land, becoming part of the rituat thingy. Getting a result they want. I don't think they should be underestimated so much as to think they don't *know* Gendo's out to doublecross them; They knew that since he had bethany base blown up. What makes you think they didn't take precautions? Hence, my theory is. At the end of Q, all three mayor factions are at a stalemate, including SEELE, who, so far, seems to be having the upper hand, allthough Gendo is confident as always. SEELEs final scene is a bit of huge latent cockfight; They're so confident they think they don't even need to be present, Gendo's so confident, he thinks he's totally lulled them into safety, and he speaks so humbly, too, "Rest assured." Of course, Gendo gets just as cocky with Kaworu and Shinji, whom he doesn't even give orders before they leave, like he's sure nothing they could possibly do could NOT benefit them (And, in fact, even while trying to thwart him, Shinji plays right into his hands) but Gendo himself in the same situation with SEELE. Discuss. SEELE, their role/significance/aesthetics as villains, and wether you think we've heard the last of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 04, 2013 10:26 pm Something about their interaction in the shutdown scene felt very "final" to me (probably because Kiel said the same parting words he said in EoE, IIRC), but I don't think we've heard the last of them in the sense of things they've left behind. Like enemy Evas in Final or Kaworu returning in some form (metaphysical or Instrumentality is my guess).

[]: Re: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:25 pm Kendrix wrote: Even before, Gendo & Fuyu were discussing how SEELE didn't need to discuss stuff with them/directly act anymore; They left behind everything already set into motion, rolling down the paths they want it on with every passing day. Everything was already set into place, and Fuyutsuki's final resumen makes it look like their faction is by far not out of worries: "It nearly went all as SEELE intended" - In fact, the old men pose most of their direct threat after their deaths, through the nifty programs they left behind in the Mk 9 and its attempt to take over the Wunder - Without it, WILLE might as well have been able to blow EVA 13 clean out of the sky.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:35 pm Well there's only two actual tables in the movie. Everything that was on Ritsuko's got thrown off by Mark.09 breaking in. And this is still on Gendo's at the end SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:39 pm monitoradiation wrote: What's Aum?

[]: Re: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Fri Oct 04, 2013 11:43 pm cyharding wrote: The Aum Shinrikyo Cult. It was a big thing back when NGE first premired.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Oct 05, 2013 12:14 am I'd say they're gone.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Oct 05, 2013 3:34 am They seem gone; but that raises the question of why Gendou didn't dispose of them earlier, since it seemed that easy. Presumably they had at that point done something that he needed them to do, and couldn't have done himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Sat Oct 05, 2013 8:27 am Following that logic, they are not dead, no. But you might also say the Founding Fathers (of the USA) are still alive because of what they set in motion, or Steve Jobs is still alive through Apple or the machines he made. All these statements are true within that logic, but it doesn't answer the questions I have. Mainly, Seele did not seem to put up any resistance to being unplugged. They didn't speak a word when Gendo announced what he was doing and one-by-one terminated them. His words were that they shouldn't worry and the plan would continue despite their absence. Did this reassure them? Assuming it did and they accepted their deaths, how can they be sure they'll benefit from the divination of mankind? At this level of analyzing the show, I don't think there's a right or wrong answer, or that we'll even see this topic brought up. Not unless we get another dump of data like the CI files. But one possibility is that NERV has produced Cores that contain the souls of the Seele members, or has otherwise preserved their souls for the moment of instrumentality. It's probably worth noting that Seele means "soul", after all. Or there is the chance that they can be resurrected after death and still be part of Instrumentality. This could apply to all humans who have died throughout history, or perhaps just Seele. Like the CI files say, Seele planned only for themselves and some elite to go with them on the Path to God. When Gendo speaks of a plan being possible "through [Seele's] deaths", he might be literal in that Seele does have to die in order to prepare for divinity. Maybe this is really some ceremony of sacrifice or martyrdom that earns them a place in "heaven", or Instrumentality. The most likely answer is that he needs Seele out of the way so he can reunite with Yui. This is the simplest answer, in my opinion. He has a plan besides instrumentality that involves only himself and Yui. Perhaps the two of them becoming a god together, or gods, or bringing Yui back as mortal.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sat Oct 05, 2013 9:22 am pwhodges wrote: They seem gone; but that raises the question of why Gendou didn't dispose of them earlier, since it seemed that easy. Presumably they had at that point done something that he needed them to do, and couldn't have done himself.

[]: Re: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 05, 2013 11:31 am monitoradiation wrote: Well the obvious implication here is that although the brains are gone, their plans are set such that their mechanization through the hardware that they left behind will ensure that their will is ultimately carried out regardless of anyone's actions. I'm kind of opposed to the deterministic thinking in the narrative sense; while it could be true, makes a story ultimately unsatisfying and somewhat pointless even in the compatibilist sense (ie., your freewill exists to the extent that your "consciousness" is able to conceptualize the idea of a choice - but how you make choices are deterministic and your freewill is in fact still illusory).

[]: Re: Is SEELE really off the table?             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sat Oct 05, 2013 11:43 am Kendrix wrote: You might think it's too deterministic, but what matters is how SEELE thinks, and they think their scenario is sufficiently set in stone by their preparations. Just see how sucessful Gendo was at getting Shinji to do what he wants - with just a slight nudge here and there... This sort of thing, sadly, sort of works in the Evaverse. SEELE think they dopn't NEED to react. Emphasis on think.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 06, 2013 2:53 pm I think that yes, they're now completely out of the picture : looking at the sorry state of the planet and how it's implied that there is very few survivor, their HIP in Q looked like a last gambit, the last chance to brought the artificial evolution upon mankind. And they did everything to put all the odds in their favor : their plan was based on two elements : that EVA-13 retrieve a LoL and that the Impact triggered by the liberation and absorption of the 12th Angel isn't stopped. Since in the end Fuyutsuki said that nearly everything has gone as SEELE wished, even through the LoC wasn't here, that meant that SEELE only really needed a LoL for HIP, and that only Kaqworu wanted a LoC with it. Whether they suspected that Gendo would try to double-cross them or not, as long as SEELE manages to accomplish these two objectives, they win. And for that they took control of the only thing that could stop EVA-13 once awakened : the AAA Wunder. And for that they installed a back-door Dummy System program which would take over Mark.09(making sure that wherever his loyalty lies Gendo won't be able to stop them) to take control of the Wunder so they won't be able to drag EVA-13 out of the vortex and shoot it to death(which Misato tried to do) If SEELE had other resources to throw at WILLE, like the Nemesis series, they would have used them against Misato, since 4I was the moment their final plan would come to fruition. And the fact not a single NS was sighted, coupled with neo-NERV's logo on the "box" in the tesseract from which the Mk.04B appeared means that it's neo-NERV which control them, not SEELE(even if it's their researches that made the existence of the units possible and they are the one who built them) On the paper, all the odds were on their favor : there was nothing anyone could have done to stop them once Mark.09 goes on auto-pilot and starts to hacks the Wunder... but there was one thing that they didn't planned Gendo would manage to do to thwart their plans : in a darkly hilarious inversion of EoE, just by changing one single object that wasn't even vital for SEELE's plans(replacing or morphing the LoC into a LoL), he made Kaworu betray his masters all of that because that wouldn't bring Shinji his happiness, the same way Gendo's plans in EoE were ultimately thwarted simply by Rei deciding that Shinji's happiness was more important than Gendo's and ditched him on the spot! And in the end, Kaworu(their main henchman) betrays them and sacrifice himself, the Wunder(their main military threat) survive the encounter and Mark.09(their main military resource and the only thing able to neutralize said main military threat) is destroyed. Even if they had spare monoliths somewhere if HIP is interrupted, they don't have any mean to influence in any way the final battle, so why bother bring them back? Q served to show us how Gendo changed into an even more ruthless and dangerous man, able to even masterfully double-cross the multi-millennium SEELE and establish him as the definitive Big Bad for the final act, even the preview was just a big teaser for the WILLE/neo-NERV fight. Bringing the old monoliths back won't serve any purpose, and besides there is already too much things to take care of to have any place left for them : there is Shinji arriving in that famous "place of hope", him learning hope again(which surely won't happen in five minutes), WILLE's and Gendo's preparations for the final battle(the reparations for the Wunder and the Evas, as well as the assemblage of the MP-EVAs won't happen in a day), and said final battle, there is simply no more space left for SEELE. They're done for, as well as the Angels, now there is only two factions left on the board, WILLE and neo-NERV, and the final battle will concern them to decide what will be mankind's fate.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Oct 06, 2013 5:53 pm ElMariachi wrote: now there is only two factions left on the board, WILLE and neo-NERV, and the final battle will concern them to decide what will be mankind's fate.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 06, 2013 6:39 pm Shinji vs Gendo, that's it if Shinji come back to fight alongside WILLE. I would really love to have a final confrontation between father and son, to have them finally tell to the other everything they have in the heart and to bring that sense of closure that really lacked in EoE, but seeing Shinji's mental state and now absolute hatred and fear of the Eva, as well as WILLE having completely lost any hope in him(if they ever had any to begin with), and that's without talking about Shinji's status as an Impact Trigger, meaning that so far with the info we have he's a walking bomb if he ever gets into an Eva, it's also possible that his participation in the final battle will be minimal(if EVA-01 decides to wake up and take Shinji with it to try to start yet another Impact), if not null(WILLE fight neo-NERV, while Shinji is slowly regaining his sanity in whatever place Asuka dumped him, but I really doubt it will happen) Or maybe Shinji will fight alongside WILLE, and Gendo will get what was coming to him by Misato herself, or Rei II and Yui, so he will finally leave Shinji alone far from his manipulations. But personally I think that such a final confrontation will happen in FINAL : unlike in NGE where Gendo begrudgingly called Shinji to pilot because Rei was wounded, in Rebuild he deliberately manipulated his son to force him to pilot, because Shinji was always part of his plan to awake EVA-01, and it's even possible that sending him to live alone with his teacher was part of Gendo's plan to mold Shinji into an insecure and solitary boy that would be intrigued by Rei and get close to her, again so they would awake Unit 01. And of course there is what happened in Q, where he horribly manipulated his son into insanity and forced him in a situation where he would have to fight his former friends just so Gendo could get rid of SEELE and get his awakened Evangelion, and it's implied that he's the one who sent Toji's shirt to Shinji which started his descent into madness and despair. So confronting Gendo would symbolize Shinji facing the demons of this past and defeating them so he could move forward again.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 06, 2013 7:49 pm pwhodges wrote: I'd say two pairings: Wille vs Nerv, but also crucially: Shinji vs Gendou.

Even before, Gendo & Fuyu were discussing how SEELE didn't need to discuss stuff wi

I would really love to have a final confrontation between father and son, to have them finally tell to the other everything they have in the heart and to bring that sense of closure that really lacked in EoE, but seeing Shinji's mental state and now absolute hatred and fear of the Eva, as well as WILLE having completely lost any hope in him(if they ever had any to begin with), and that's without talking about Shinji's status as an Impact Trigger, meaning that so far with the info we have he's a walking bomb if he ever gets into an Eva, it's also possible that his participation in the final battle will be minimal(if EVA-01 decides to wake up and take Shinji with it to try to start yet another Impact), if not null(WILLE fight neo-NERV, while Shinji is slowly regaining his sanity in whatever place Asuka dumped him, but I really doubt it will happen) Or maybe Shinji will fight alongside WILLE, and Gendo will get what was coming to him by Misato herself, or Rei II and Yui, so he will finally leave Shinji alone far from his manipulations. But personally I think that such a final confrontation will happen in FINAL : unlike in NGE where Gendo begrudgingly called Shinji to pilot because Rei was wounded, in Rebuild he deliberately manipulated his son to force him to pilot, because Shinji was always part of his plan to awake EVA-01, and it's even possible that sending him to live alone with his teacher was part of Gendo's plan to mold Shinji into an insecure and solitary boy that would be intrigued by Rei and get close to her, again so they would awake Unit 01. And of course there is what happened in Q, where he horribly manipulated his son into insanity and forced him in a situation where he would have to fight his former friends just so Gendo could get rid of SEELE and get his awakened Evangelion, and it's implied that he's the one who sent Toji's shirt to Shinji which started his descent into madness and despair. So confronting Gendo would symbolize Shinji facing the demons of this past and defeating them so he could move forward again.
